Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the two-dimensional vectors a and b are given. a) Find the measure of the angle $$\theta$$ between a and b . Express the answer in radians rounded to two decimal places, if it is not possible to express it exactly. b) Is $$\theta$$ an acute angle?
Question: [T] $$\mathbf{\text{a}} = \langle 3 , -1 \rangle ,$$ $$\textbf{b} = \langle -4 , 0 \rangle$$
Answer: a. $$\theta = 2.82$$ rad; b. $$\theta$$ is not acute.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises determine whether the given vectors are orthogonal.
Question: Find all two-dimensional vectors a orthogonal to vector $$\textbf{b} = \langle 3 , 4 \rangle .$$ Express the answer in component form.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises determine whether the given vectors are orthogonal.
Question: Find all two-dimensional vectors a orthogonal to vector $$\textbf{b} = \langle 5 , -6 \rangle .$$ Express the answer by using standard unit vectors.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises determine whether the given vectors are orthogonal.
Question: Determine all three-dimensional vectors $$\textbf{u}$$ orthogonal to vector $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \langle 1 , 1 , 0 \rangle .$$ Express the answer by using standard unit vectors.
Answer: $$\text{u} = - \alpha \text{i} + \alpha \textbf{j} + \beta \text{k} ,$$ where $$\alpha$$ and $$\beta$$ are real numbers such that $$\alpha^{2} + \beta^{2} \ne 0$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises determine whether the given vectors are orthogonal.
Question: Determine all three-dimensional vectors $$\textbf{u}$$ orthogonal to vector $$\text{v} = \text{i} - \text{j} - \textbf{k} .$$ Express the answer in component form.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises determine whether the given vectors are orthogonal.
Question: Determine the real number $$\alpha$$ such that vectors $$\text{a} = 2 \text{i} + 3 \textbf{j}$$ and $$\textbf{b} = 9 \text{i} + \alpha \textbf{j}$$ are orthogonal.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises determine whether the given vectors are orthogonal.
Question: Determine the real number $$\alpha$$ such that vectors $$\text{a} = -3 \text{i} + 2 \textbf{j}$$ and $$\textbf{b} = 2 \text{i} + \alpha \textbf{j}$$ are orthogonal.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises determine whether the given vectors are orthogonal.
Question: [T] Consider the points $$P ( 4 , 5 )$$ and $$Q ( 5 , -7 ) .$$ Determine vectors $$\vec{O P}$$ and $$\vec{O Q} .$$ Express the answer by using standard unit vectors. Determine the measure of angle O in triangle OPQ . Express the answer in degrees rounded to two decimal places.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises determine whether the given vectors are orthogonal.
Question: [T] Consider points $$A ( 1 , 1 ) ,$$ $$B ( 2 , -7 ) ,$$ and $$C ( 6 , 3 ) .$$ Determine vectors $$\vec{B A}$$ and $$\vec{B C} .$$ Express the answer in component form. Determine the measure of angle B in triangle ABC . Express the answer in degrees rounded to two decimal places.

Question: Determine the measure of angle A in triangle ABC , where $$A ( 1 , 1 , 8 ) ,$$ $$B ( 4 , -3 , -4 ) ,$$ and $$C ( -3 , 1 , 5 ) .$$ Express your answer in degrees rounded to two decimal places.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises determine whether the given vectors are orthogonal.
Question: Consider points $$P ( 3 , 7 , -2 )$$ and $$Q ( 1 , 1 , -3 ) .$$ Determine the angle between vectors $$\vec{O P}$$ and $$\vec{O Q} .$$ Express the answer in degrees rounded to two decimal places.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine which (if any) pairs of the following vectors are orthogonal.
Question: $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = \langle 3 , 7 , -2 \rangle ,$$ $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \langle 5 , -3 , -3 \rangle ,$$ $$\mathbf{\text{w}} = \langle 0 , 1 , -1 \rangle$$
Answer: $$\textbf{u}$$ and $$\textbf{v}$$ are orthogonal; $$\textbf{v}$$ and $$\textbf{w}$$ are orthogonal.

Question: Use vectors to show that a parallelogram with equal diagonals is a rectangle.

Question: Use vectors to show that the diagonals of a rhombus are perpendicular.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine which (if any) pairs of the following vectors are orthogonal.
Question: Show that $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \cdotp ( \mathbf{\text{v}} + \mathbf{\text{w}} ) = \mathbf{\text{u}} \cdotp \mathbf{\text{v}} + \mathbf{\text{u}} \cdotp \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ is true for any vectors $$\textbf{u}$$ , $$\textbf{v}$$ , and $$\textbf{w}$$ .

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, determine which (if any) pairs of the following vectors are orthogonal.
Question: Verify the identity $$\mathbf{\text{u}} \cdotp ( \mathbf{\text{v}} + \mathbf{\text{w}} ) = \mathbf{\text{u}} \cdotp \mathbf{\text{v}} + \mathbf{\text{u}} \cdotp \mathbf{\text{w}}$$ for vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = \langle 1 , 0 , 4 \rangle ,$$ $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = \langle -2 , 3 , 5 \rangle ,$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{w}} = \langle 4 , -2 , 6 \rangle .$$

Question: Consider the vectors $$\mathbf{\text{u}} = 4 \mathbf{\text{i}} - 3 \mathbf{\text{j}}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{v}} = 3 \mathbf{\text{i}} + 2 \mathbf{\text{j}} .$$ Find the component form of vector $$\text{w} = \left(\text{proj}\right)_{\textbf{u}} \textbf{v}$$ that represents the projection of $$\textbf{v}$$ onto $$\textbf{u}$$ . Write the decomposition $$\text{v} = \textbf{w} + \textbf{q}$$ of vector $$\textbf{v}$$ into the orthogonal components $$\textbf{w}$$ and $$\textbf{q}$$ , where $$\textbf{w}$$ is the projection of $$\textbf{v}$$ onto $$\textbf{u}$$ and $$\textbf{q}$$ is a vector orthogonal to the direction of $$\textbf{u}$$ .

Write the decomposition $$\text{v} = \textbf{w} + \textbf{q}$$ of vector $$\textbf{v}$$ into the orthogonal components $$\textbf{w}$$ and $$\textbf{q}$$ , where $$\textbf{w}$$ is the projection of $$\textbf{v}$$ onto $$\textbf{u}$$ and $$\textbf{q}$$ is a vector orthogonal to the direction of $$\textbf{u}$$ .

Question: A methane molecule has a carbon atom situated at the origin and four hydrogen atoms located at points $$P ( 1 , 1 , -1 ) , Q ( 1 , -1 , 1 ) , R ( -1 , 1 , 1 ) , \text{and} S ( -1 , -1 , -1 )$$ (see figure). Find the distance between the hydrogen atoms located at P and R . Find the angle between vectors $$\vec{O S}$$ and $$\vec{O R}$$ that connect the carbon atom with the hydrogen atoms located at S and R , which is also called the bond angle . Express the answer in degrees rounded to two decimal places.

Express your answer in component form. b) Find the scalar projection $$\left(\text{comp}\right)_{\textbf{u}} \textbf{v}$$ of vector $$\textbf{v}$$ onto vector u.

Express your answer in component form. b) Find the scalar projection $$\left(\text{comp}\right)_{\textbf{u}} \textbf{v}$$ of vector $$\textbf{v}$$ onto vector u.

Express your answer in component form. b) Find the scalar projection $$\left(\text{comp}\right)_{\textbf{u}} \textbf{v}$$ of vector $$\textbf{v}$$ onto vector u.
Question: [T] A sled is pulled by exerting a force of 100 N on a rope that makes an angle of $$25 \text{°}$$ with the horizontal. Find the work done in pulling the sled 40 m. (Round the answer to one decimal place.)

Express your answer in component form. b) Find the scalar projection $$\left(\text{comp}\right)_{\textbf{u}} \textbf{v}$$ of vector $$\textbf{v}$$ onto vector u.
Question: [T] A father is pulling his son on a sled at an angle of $$20 \text{°}$$ with the horizontal with a force of 25 lb (see the following image). He pulls the sled in a straight path of 50 ft. How much work was done by the man pulling the sled? (Round the answer to the nearest integer.)

Question: [T] A car is towed using a force of 1600 N. The rope used to pull the car makes an angle of 25° with the horizontal. Find the work done in towing the car 2 km. Express the answer in joules $$( 1 \text{J} = 1 \text{N} \cdotp \text{m} )$$ rounded to the nearest integer.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the vectors $$\textbf{u}$$ and $$\textbf{v}$$ are given. a) Find the vector projection $$\textbf{w} = \left(\text{proj}\right)_{\text{u}} \text{v}$$ of vector $$\textbf{v}$$ onto vector $$\textbf{u}$$ . Question: [T] A boat sails north aided by a wind blowing in a direction of $$\text{N3} 0 \text{°} \text{E}$$ with a magnitude of 500 lb. How much work is performed by the wind as the boat moves 100 ft? (Round the answer to two decimal places.)

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the vectors $$\textbf{u}$$ and $$\textbf{v}$$ are given. a) Find the vector projection $$\textbf{w} = \left(\text{proj}\right)_{\text{u}} \text{v}$$ of vector $$\textbf{v}$$ onto vector $$\textbf{u}$$ . Express your answer in component form. b) Find the scalar projection $$\left(\text{comp}\right)_{\textbf{u}} \textbf{v}$$ of vector $$\textbf{v}$$ onto vector u.
Question: Vector $$\mathbf{\text{p}} = \langle 150 , 225 , 375 \rangle$$ represents the price of certain models of bicycles sold by a bicycle shop. Vector $$\mathbf{\text{n}} = \langle 10 , 7 , 9 \rangle$$ represents the number of bicycles sold of each model, respectively. Compute the dot product $$\mathbf{\text{p}} \cdotp \mathbf{\text{n}}$$ and state its meaning.

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the vectors $$\textbf{u}$$ and $$\textbf{v}$$ are given. a) Find the vector projection $$\textbf{w} = \left(\text{proj}\right)_{\text{u}} \text{v}$$ of vector $$\textbf{v}$$ onto vector $$\textbf{u}$$ . Express your answer in component form. b) Find the scalar projection $$\left(\text{comp}\right)_{\textbf{u}} \textbf{v}$$ of vector $$\textbf{v}$$ onto vector u.
Question: [T] Two forces $$\mathbf{\text{F}}_{1}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{F}}_{2}$$ are represented by vectors with initial points that are at the origin. The first force has a magnitude of 20 lb and the terminal point of the vector is point $$P ( 1 , 1 , 0 ) .$$ The second force has a magnitude of 40 lb and the terminal point of its vector is point $$Q ( 0 , 1 , 1 ) .$$ Let F be the resultant force of forces $$\mathbf{\text{F}}_{1}$$ and $$\mathbf{\text{F}}_{2} .$$ Find the magnitude of F . (Round the answer to one decimal place.) Find the direction angles of F . (Express the answer in degrees rounded to one decimal place.)
Answer: a. $$\parallel \left(\text{F}\right)_{1} + \left(\textbf{F}\right)_{2} \parallel = 52.9$$ lb; b. The direction angles are $$\alpha = 74.5 \text{°} ,$$ $$\beta = 36.7 \text{°} ,$$ and $$\gamma = 57.7 \text{°} .$$

Problem Statement: For the following exercises, the vectors $$\textbf{u}$$ and $$\textbf{v}$$ are given. a) Find the vector projection $$\textbf{w} = \left(\text{proj}\right)_{\text{u}} \text{v}$$ of vector $$\textbf{v}$$ onto vector $$\textbf{u}$$ . Express your answer in component form. b) Find the scalar projection $$\left(\text{comp}\right)_{\textbf{u}} \textbf{v}$$ of vector $$\textbf{v}$$ onto vector u.
Question: [T] Consider $$\textbf{r} \left(\right. t \left.\right) = \langle \text{cos} t , \text{sin} t , 2 t \rangle$$ the position vector of a particle at time $$t \in [ 0 , 30 ] ,$$ where the components of r are expressed in centimeters and time in seconds. Let $$\vec{O P}$$ be the position vector of the particle after 1 sec. Show that all vectors $$\vec{P Q} ,$$ where $$Q ( x , y , z )$$ is an arbitrary point, orthogonal to the instantaneous velocity vector $$\mathbf{\text{v}} ( 1 )$$ of the particle after 1 sec, can be expressed as $$\vec{P Q} = \langle x - \text{cos} 1 , y - \text{sin} 1 , z - 2 \rangle ,$$ where $$x \text{sin} 1 - y \text{cos} 1 - 2 z + 4 = 0 .$$ The set of point Q describes a plane called the normal plane to the path of the particle at point P . Use a CAS to visualize the instantaneous velocity vector and the normal plane at point P along with the path of the particle.
